title: test parse
provider-pool-resilience: Fix & harden all 13 provider-pool / round-robin / failover findings from deep analysis
design: Fix two structural bugs: (1) OpenAICompatibleService.translate() swallows ApiError into {success:false} where ProviderPoolCoordinator.dispatchWithFailover expects a thrown error -> circuit-breaker + failover are dead code in production (stub tests masked it). Fix: re-throw ApiError on transport/auth/rate-limit; keep {success:false} for content/parse failures. (2) PoolCursor advances in [0,slots.length) but dispatch indexes the filtered healthy[] subset -> skewed distribution + same-slot repeats. Fix: cursor over healthy pool's own index space. Plus: response_format memory keyed by baseUrl+model (survives rebuild), rate-limiter acquire() deadline bounded by requestTimeoutMs, hot-path config dirty-tracking (skip rebuild+updateConfig+AES-decrypt when settings unchanged), page-path cache guard for partial back-fills, PoolExhaustedError non-null lastError, pool testConnection skips open slots, double-retry layering bounded.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: real OpenAICompatibleService 429 from slot1 opens breaker + fails over to slot2 (integration test, mocked fetch, not throwing stub). AC2: even round-robin distribution when a slot is open (no skew/repeats). AC3: response_format stays disabled across rebuild when baseUrl+model unchanged. AC4: all-slots-fail error is non-null + descriptive. AC5: page path never caches partial back-fill. AC6: repeated translate() without settings change skips rebuild/updateConfig/decrypt. AC7: pnpm lint clean, pnpm test --run green, wxt build succeeds. AC8: no regression in 107 prior pool tests.
close_reason: All 8 phases complete. 13 findings fixed, 1723 tests pass, 0 regressions. Multi-key circuit-breaker failover now works in production.

title: BUG: Popup uses legacy provider readiness, shows 'setup not successful' when pool is ready
description:
ROOT CAUSE of 'popup shows setup not successful'.

entrypoints/popup/App.tsx:886 calls getProviderReadiness(settings.provider) — the LEGACY single-provider readiness which gates canTranslate on provider.connectionStatus === 'success'. But the new ProvidersSection UI writes to settings.providers[] (the pool) and never sets connectionStatus on the legacy mirror. So a user who configures a working provider in the Providers tab has a healthy pool, yet the popup sees the stale legacy mirror (connectionStatus='unknown') and shows the amber 'Provider not ready / Set up provider' banner, hiding the translate button.

The pool-aware getPoolReadinessStatus() was already written for exactly this (commit e4276e2, lib/providerReadiness.ts:208-260) and returns canTranslate:true (reason:'pool-ready') when a dispatchable (provider,key) slot exists — no connection test required. The ProvidersSection.tsx:81 banner already uses it. The popup was never migrated.

FIX: Switch popup to getPoolReadinessStatus(settings) + getPoolRecoveryMessage(). Keep the legacy getProviderReadiness for the wizard's own legacy mirror if needed. Add a popup test asserting pool-ready → no recovery banner + translate button visible.

Note: Bug is purely cosmetic/gating — translation itself is dispatched by the coordinator from settings.providers (Bug A is the actual dispatch failure). But fixing this is required for the symptom to clear.

title: BUG: ProviderPoolCoordinator.rebuild() does not propagate config changes to preserved member services
description:
ROOT CAUSE of 'Test works but translate fails'.

services/providerPool.ts rebuild() (lines 148-163) preserves the OpenAICompatibleService member instance when a key's id is unchanged, and updates existing.slot — but NEVER calls member.service.updateConfig(slot.providerConfig). The member service keeps its own private this.config (constructed at services/openaiCompatible.ts:43) from first construction.

REPRO: User sets up a provider in ProvidersSection (writes settings.providers[]). Test button works (it builds a FRESH ProviderConfig from UI state and calls providerTester.testConnection directly, bypassing the coordinator). But actual page translation dispatches through the coordinator's STALE member service, sending requests with the old/wrong apiKey/baseUrl/model → fails (401/404/network) and circuit-breaker opens.

FIX: In rebuild(), when existing member is preserved, call member.service.updateConfig(slot.providerConfig) so the OpenAI service reconfigures itself (config + rate limiter maxRpm + resets responseFormatDisabled). Guard for services that don't implement updateConfig (the TranslationService interface doesn't require it — only OpenAICompatibleService has it). Consider widening the interface.

TEST GAP: services/__tests__/providerPool.test.ts:124-138 only tests instance preservation with IDENTICAL settings. Needs a test: rebuild twice with the SAME keyId but DIFFERENT config, assert the member service received the new config (via updateConfig being called, or via dispatch using new values).

title: Task: Conductor - User Manual Verification 'Phase 4: Rate-Limiter Timeout Coupling'

design: acquire(timeoutMs?) — if computed wait exceeds budget, reject with typed RateLimitTimeoutError. Keep unlimited fast-path. Files: lib/rateLimiter.ts

title: Task 4.3: Wire request timeout into acquire() from fetchWithRetry
design: Pass requestTimeoutMs as the acquire deadline so a low-maxRpm under load surfaces a clear error instead of hanging past the user bound. Files: services/openaiCompatible.ts
acceptance_criteria: Acquire respects the configured timeout.

title: Task 4.1: Test acquire() respects a deadline
design: RED: cap=1, fill window, acquire() with short deadline -> must reject (typed error) instead of waiting indefinitely. Use fake timers. Files: lib/__tests__/rateLimiter.test.ts
acceptance_criteria: Test fails on current impl, passes after 4.2.

title: Phase 4: Rate-Limiter Timeout Coupling (FR-5) — bound acquire() by requestTimeoutMs; fixes #4

COMMIT: de6aa11
FILES: lib/rateLimiter.ts, services/openaiCompatible.ts, lib/__tests__/rateLimiter.test.ts, services/__tests__/openaiCompatible.test.ts
KEY: acquire(timeoutMs?) rejects with typed RateLimitTimeoutError when deadline exceeded; fetchWithRetry passes requestTimeoutMs as the deadline. value: PDF Download feature (pdf-download_20260618): Uses pdf-lib + @pdf-lib/fontkit for PDF generation. fontBytes is optional in translatedPdfGenerator (falls back to Helvetica). Font cached in IndexedDB via idb-keyval with key 'pdf-font:noto-sans:v1'. translateAllPages processes pages sequentially with per-page error isolation. usePdfDownload hook orchestrates 3-stage pipeline: translate-all → font fetch → PDF generation → browser download via anchor click. Noto Sans TTF fetched from fonts.gstatic.com with streaming progress. vi.stubGlobal returns previous value, not the spy — create vi.fn() separately.
